normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU1_input_precond
  (n : Nat) (source : Nat) (edges : Graph)
  (h : ∀ e ∈ edges, let s := e.1; let t := e.2.1; let w := e.2.2; s < n ∧ t < n ∧ w ≥ 0) :
  NonNegativeWeights edges := by
  -- proof obligation: package the edge-range + non-negativity assumption as a reusable predicate
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input precondition: all edge endpoints are within [0,n) and all weights are non-negative.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU2_length
  (n : Nat) (source : Nat) (edges : Graph) : (dijkstra n source edges).length = n := by
  -- proof obligation
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned distance vector has length n.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_source_dist
  (n : Nat) (source : Nat) (edges : Graph)
  (hs : source < n) : (dijkstra n source edges).get? source = some (some 0) := by
  -- proof obligation
  admit

theorem SU4_achievable
  (n : Nat) (source : Nat) (edges : Graph)
  (v : Nat) (d : Nat)
  (hv : v < n)
  (hd : (dijkstra n source edges).get? v = some (some d)) :
  PathWeight edges source v d := by
  -- proof obligation: witness path exists with total edge weight exactly d
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(d), then there exists a path from source to v with total weight d.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU5_optimal
  (n : Nat) (source : Nat) (edges : Graph)
  (v : Nat) (d : Nat)
  (hW : NonNegativeWeights edges)
  (hv : v < n)
  (hd : (dijkstra n source edges).get? v = some (some d)) :
  ∀ w, PathWeight edges source v w → d ≤ w := by
  -- proof obligation: no cheaper path exists
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = Some(d), then no path from source to v has total weight less than d.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU6_unreachable
  (n : Nat) (source : Nat) (edges : Graph)
  (v : Nat)
  (hv : v < n)
  (hu : (dijkstra n source edges).get? v = some none) :
  ¬ ∃ w, PathWeight edges source v w := by
  -- proof obligation: unreachable means no path exists in the graph sense
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If dist[v] = None (within range), then there is no path from source to v.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU7_termination
  (n : Nat) (source : Nat) (edges : Graph) : ∃ dist : DistTable, dist = dijkstra n source edges := by
  refine ⟨dijkstra n source edges, rfl⟩
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The algorithm terminates on every valid input (the function returns a value).
